algorithms.title: Explosive Event Scene Sequence
algorithms.description: Responder priorities at an intentional or suspected-intentional explosion.
steps: Stage at a safe distance; assume secondary devices until law enforcement clears the scene.
Establish/join unified command with law enforcement and fire.
Enter per the coordinated plan; preserve evidence where care allows.
Apply SALT triage; the dominant lifesaving intervention is hemorrhage control (tourniquets, packing).
Anticipate the casualty pattern: many fragment injuries, occult primary blast injury in survivors near/inside the blast space.
Warn receiving hospitals early and distribute casualties; flag enclosed-space survivors for observation.
Reassess the scene continuously for structural, fire, and device hazards.

algorithms.title: Blast Lung Recognition and Care
algorithms.description: Detecting and safely managing primary pulmonary blast injury.
steps: Suspect in any significant blast exposure — especially enclosed-space — with dyspnea, hypoxia, chest pain, or hemoptysis (may be delayed hours).
Administer high-flow oxygen; monitor SpO2 continuously.
If ventilation is required, use the lowest effective pressures/volumes.
Monitor for tension pneumothorax; decompress if it develops.
Consider arterial air embolism with neuro deficits or sudden collapse.
Observe significant exposures even when initially asymptomatic.
